channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Commission finds October 29 election violence in Tanzania was organised and funded operation
item.description: Tanzania’s Commission of Inquiry into the October 29, 2025 post-election violence has concluded that the incidents were not spontaneous or peaceful demonstrations, but rather a well-organised operatio...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Orders Inquiry Over Non-Functional Weather Warning Systems in GB
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has expressed strong concern over the non-functional early weather warning systems in Gilgit-Baltistan, which are crucial for protection against climate hazards such as g...

item.title: Raiders' Error-Riddled Loss to Tigers Highlights Key Issues Ahead of Key Match
item.description: Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart blasted his team's performance after a costly 33-14 loss to the Wests Tigers at Leichhardt Oval. Despite leading 14-10 at halftime, the Raiders fell apart in the se...

item.title: Pakistan rejects India’s accusations over Pahalgam incident, urges responsible behaviour
item.description: The Foreign Office of Pakistan has strongly condemned India for spreading baseless allegations linking Pakistan with the April 2025 Pahalgam incident. Islamabad has urged New Delhi to act responsibly ...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Shares Her Side After Video Shows Damage at Host’s House in the US
item.description: Ann Kioko, a Kenyan woman whose video went viral for allegedly damaging property at a host’s house in the US, has spoken out to clarify her side of the story. The video showed Ann allegedly intoxicate...

item.title: Extreme Heat Poses Serious Risks for Pregnant Women in Karachi
item.description: In Karachi, pregnant women are facing severe health challenges due to extreme heat and poor living conditions. Many, like Asiya from Lyari, experience exhaustion, nausea, and fear for their babies' he...

item.title: Sindh authorities suspend three SHOs and launch inquiry after deadly alleged drone incident in Khairpur
item.description: In Khairpur district of Sindh, an alleged drone strike in Saeed Khan Lakho village resulted in the deaths of four civilians, prompting immediate action from provincial authorities. Sindh Home Minister...

item.title: Taraji P Henson discusses Broadway debut, burnout, and Black resilience
item.description: Taraji P. Henson, an Oscar-nominated actress, is making her Broadway debut in August Wilson's 'Joe Turner's Come and Gone.' In an interview, Henson opens up about her career in Hollywood, the struggle...

item.title: Former Chapel Hill officer arrested in Florida over alleged plot targeting New Orleans festival
item.description: A former police officer has been arrested in Florida after authorities say they uncovered evidence suggesting he was planning a mass shooting at a festival in New Orleans, according to the Okaloosa Co...

item.title: Daisy Thomas and Jobe Watson Share Their Experiences of Playing on Anzac Day
item.description: The Anzac Day clash between Collingwood and Essendon is more than just an AFL game; it is a significant event that embodies Australian culture and national pride. Former Essendon captain Jobe Watson a...

item.title: Impact of March 2026 Google Core Update on Digital Government Media SEO
item.description: In April 2026, Google concluded its first core update of the year, which was deployed in late March. This update affected digital government media, emphasizing the importance of content authority and ...

item.title: Jennette McCurdy Explores Age-Gap Relationships and Power Dynamics in Debut Novel
item.description: Jennette McCurdy, known for her work as a former child star, has released her debut novel 'Half His Age', which examines age-gap relationships and abuse of power in educational settings. The story rev...

item.title: Celebrities and watchmakers converge at Geneva’s Watches and Wonders fair
item.description: The Watches and Wonders event in Geneva once again cemented its reputation as the premier global showcase for luxury watchmaking, drawing a mix of high-profile celebrities, industry leaders, and inter...

item.title: An Overview of the 2026 Turner Prize Nominees and Their Artistic Approaches
item.description: The 2026 Turner Prize shortlist has been met with observations that it is noticeably more cautious compared to past years. Unlike previous editions, this year's selection lacks older artists, painters...

item.title: US shipbuilding constraints raise fresh doubts over AUKUS submarine delivery timelines
item.description: The article reports growing concerns about delays in the AUKUS submarine program despite significant financial commitments from Australia and the United States. According to new analysis prepared for ...

item.title: Faith Leaders and Government Unite to Improve Family Health Services in Kenya
item.description: In Nairobi, a major initiative called the Interfaith National Hub on Family Health and Wellbeing has been launched to tackle persistent healthcare gaps in Kenya. Spearheaded by the Faith to Action Net...

item.title: Kenya Shuts Over 1,200 Health Facilities Amid SHA and NHIF Fraud Investigations
item.description: Health CS Aden Duale has confirmed that over 1,200 health facilities have been closed down as part of the government’s intensified efforts to fight fraud linked to the Social Health Authority (SHA) an...

item.title: Experts highlight expectations and challenges as Taiwo Oyedele becomes Nigeria’s Finance Minister
item.description: Economic experts don share their views on wetin Taiwo Oyedele appointment as Nigeria new Minister of Finance and Coordinating Minister of the Economy fit mean for the country. Dem point to im strong b...

item.title: Canadian Rangers test communications, technology, and operations during extended Arctic patrol along the Northwest Passage
item.description: A 52-day Canadian Ranger patrol covering approximately 5,200 kilometres along the Northwest Passage has highlighted both the human and technological challenges of operating in Canada’s Arctic. Led by ...

item.title: Opinion: Carney criticized for attributing domestic economic challenges to Trump amid housing and debt concerns
item.description: This opinion article argues that Prime Minister Mark Carney is deflecting responsibility for Canada’s domestic economic challenges by attributing them in part to U.S. President Donald Trump, rather th...
